issues.description: SessionResumptionConfig is now always included in LiveConnectConfig for all GeminiLive sessions, including live.py's endpoint. This means session_resumption_update events will be forwarded to live.py clients via the catch-all else branch in forward_gemini_event. Not a breaking change (clients ignore unrecognized message types), but slightly changes the behavior of the existing live endpoint without explicit documentation.
No test for edge case where update.resumable is True but update.new_handle is None/empty. The current guard (update.resumable and update.new_handle) correctly suppresses this, but it's untested.
In brainstorm.py forward_gemini_event, the explicit 'session_resumption_update' case does the same thing as the else branch (send_to_client(event)). Redundant but acceptable for clarity/readability.

sharedStateObservations.description: voidpilot-knowledge.md should document that SessionResumptionConfig is now always included in LiveConnectConfig for all GeminiLive sessions (not just brainstorm). Workers modifying session config should be aware this affects all endpoints.

Worker's skillFeedback.suggestedChanges notes that testing GeminiLive.start_session internals requires careful mock setup with call_count-based receive mocks to prevent infinite loops. This is useful knowledge for the fullstack-worker skill's test guidance section.

summary: Session resumption implementation is clean, correct, and well-tested. All three assertions (BE-SR-01, BE-SR-02, BE-SR-03) are fulfilled. The GeminiLive class properly accepts session_resumption_handle with None default, includes SessionResumptionConfig in LiveConnectConfig, and yields session_resumption_update events when resumable+new_handle are truthy. Brainstorm endpoint correctly sets handle from session_config messages and forwards events. live.py is unchanged and backward compatible. 10 new tests cover constructor params, config construction, event yielding, negative cases, and brainstorm integration. No blocking issues found.
